signing.c(3) Library Functions Manual signing.c(3)

NAME

signing.c - GPG signing operations implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int gpg_sign_with_key (const char *key_id, const uint8_t *message, size_t message_len, uint8_t *signature_out, size_t *signature_len_out)
Sign a message using GPG key (via gpg --detach-sign). int gpg_sign_detached_ed25519 (const char *key_id, const uint8_t *message, size_t message_len, uint8_t signature_out[64])

Detailed Description

GPG signing operations implementation.

Definition in file signing.c.

Function Documentation

int gpg_sign_detached_ed25519 (const char * key_id, const uint8_t * message, size_t message_len, uint8_t signature_out[64])

Definition at line 144 of file signing.c.

145                                                          {
146   log_debug("gpg_sign_detached_ed25519: Signing with key ID %s (fallback mode)", key_id);
147 
148   // Get OpenPGP signature packet from gpg --detach-sign
149   uint8_t openpgp_signature[512];
150   size_t openpgp_len = 0;
151 
152   int result = gpg_sign_with_key(key_id, message, message_len, openpgp_signature, &openpgp_len);
153   if (result != 0) {
154     log_error("GPG detached signing failed for key %s", key_id);
155     return -1;
156   }
157 
158   log_debug("gpg_sign_with_key returned %zu bytes", openpgp_len);
159 
160   if (openpgp_len < 10) {
161     log_error("GPG signature too short: %zu bytes", openpgp_len);
162     return -1;
163   }
164 
165   log_debug("Parsing OpenPGP signature packet (%zu bytes) to extract Ed25519 signature", openpgp_len);
166 
167   // Parse OpenPGP signature packet format
168   // Reference: RFC 4880 Section 5.2 (Signature Packet)
169   // Format: [header][version][type][algo][hash-algo][...][signature-data]
170   size_t offset = 0;
171 
172   // Parse packet header
173   uint8_t tag = openpgp_signature[offset++];
174   size_t packet_len = 0;
175 
176   if ((tag & 0x40) == 0) {
177     // Old format packet
178     uint8_t length_type = tag & 0x03;
179     if (length_type == 0) {
180       packet_len = openpgp_signature[offset++];
181     } else if (length_type == 1) {
182       packet_len = (openpgp_signature[offset] << 8) | openpgp_signature[offset + 1];
183       offset += 2;
184     } else if (length_type == 2) {
185       packet_len = (openpgp_signature[offset] << 24) | (openpgp_signature[offset + 1] << 16) |
186                    (openpgp_signature[offset + 2] << 8) | openpgp_signature[offset + 3];
187       offset += 4;
188     } else {
189       log_error("Unsupported old-format packet length type: %d", length_type);
190       return -1;
191     }
192   } else {
193     // New format packet
194     uint8_t length_byte = openpgp_signature[offset++];
195     if (length_byte < 192) {
196       packet_len = length_byte;
197     } else if (length_byte < 224) {
198       packet_len = ((length_byte - 192) << 8) + openpgp_signature[offset++] + 192;
199     } else if (length_byte == 255) {
200       packet_len = (openpgp_signature[offset] << 24) | (openpgp_signature[offset + 1] << 16) |
201                    (openpgp_signature[offset + 2] << 8) | openpgp_signature[offset + 3];
202       offset += 4;
203     } else {
204       log_error("Unsupported new-format packet length encoding: %d", length_byte);
205       return -1;
206     }
207   }
208 
209   if (offset + packet_len > openpgp_len) {
210     log_error("Packet length exceeds signature size: %zu + %zu > %zu", offset, packet_len, openpgp_len);
211     return -1;
212   }
213 
214   log_debug("Signature packet: offset=%zu, length=%zu", offset, packet_len);
215 
216   // Parse signature packet body
217   // Skip: version (1), sig_type (1), pub_algo (1), hash_algo (1)
218   if (offset + 4 > openpgp_len) {
219     log_error("Signature packet too short for header");
220     return -1;
221   }
222 
223   uint8_t version = openpgp_signature[offset++];
224   uint8_t sig_type = openpgp_signature[offset++];
225   uint8_t pub_algo = openpgp_signature[offset++];
226   uint8_t hash_algo = openpgp_signature[offset++];
227 
228   log_debug("Signature: version=%d, type=%d, algo=%d, hash=%d", version, sig_type, pub_algo, hash_algo);
229 
230   // Verify algorithm is Ed25519 (22 = EdDSA)
231   if (pub_algo != 22) {
232     log_error("Expected EdDSA algorithm (22), got %d", pub_algo);
233     return -1;
234   }
235 
236   // For v4 signatures: skip hashed subpackets
237   if (version == 4) {
238     if (offset + 2 > openpgp_len) {
239       log_error("Cannot read hashed subpacket length");
240       return -1;
241     }
242     uint16_t hashed_len = (openpgp_signature[offset] << 8) | openpgp_signature[offset + 1];
243     offset += 2;
244     offset += hashed_len; // Skip hashed subpackets
245 
246     if (offset + 2 > openpgp_len) {
247       log_error("Cannot read unhashed subpacket length");
248       return -1;
249     }
250     uint16_t unhashed_len = (openpgp_signature[offset] << 8) | openpgp_signature[offset + 1];
251     offset += 2;
252     offset += unhashed_len; // Skip unhashed subpackets
253 
254     // Skip left 16 bits of signed hash value
255     if (offset + 2 > openpgp_len) {
256       log_error("Cannot read hash left bits");
257       return -1;
258     }
259     offset += 2;
260   }
261 
262   // Now we're at the signature data (MPI format for Ed25519)
263   // Ed25519 signature is: r (32 bytes) || s (32 bytes) = 64 bytes total
264   // In OpenPGP, each MPI is encoded as: [2-byte bit count][data]
265 
266   if (offset + 2 > openpgp_len) {
267     log_error("Cannot read MPI bit count for R");
268     return -1;
269   }
270 
271   uint16_t r_bits = (openpgp_signature[offset] << 8) | openpgp_signature[offset + 1];
272   offset += 2;
273   size_t r_bytes = (r_bits + 7) / 8;
274 
275   log_debug("R: %d bits (%zu bytes)", r_bits, r_bytes);
276 
277   if (r_bytes != 32) {
278     log_error("Expected 32-byte R value, got %zu bytes", r_bytes);
279     return -1;
280   }
281 
282   if (offset + r_bytes > openpgp_len) {
283     log_error("R value exceeds packet size");
284     return -1;
285   }
286 
287   memcpy(signature_out, &openpgp_signature[offset], 32);
288   offset += r_bytes;
289 
290   // Read S value
291   if (offset + 2 > openpgp_len) {
292     log_error("Cannot read MPI bit count for S");
293     return -1;
294   }
295 
296   uint16_t s_bits = (openpgp_signature[offset] << 8) | openpgp_signature[offset + 1];
297   offset += 2;
298   size_t s_bytes = (s_bits + 7) / 8;
299 
300   log_debug("S: %d bits (%zu bytes)", s_bits, s_bytes);
301 
302   if (s_bytes != 32) {
303     log_error("Expected 32-byte S value, got %zu bytes", s_bytes);
304     return -1;
305   }
306 
307   if (offset + s_bytes > openpgp_len) {
308     log_error("S value exceeds packet size");
309     return -1;
310   }
311 
312   memcpy(signature_out + 32, &openpgp_signature[offset], 32);
313 
314   log_debug("Successfully extracted 64-byte Ed25519 signature from OpenPGP packet");
315 
316   // Debug: Log signature components
317   char hex_r[65], hex_s[65];
318   for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
319     safe_snprintf(hex_r + i * 2, 3, "%02x", signature_out[i]);
320     safe_snprintf(hex_s + i * 2, 3, "%02x", signature_out[i + 32]);
321   }
322   hex_r[64] = hex_s[64] = '\0';
323   log_debug("Signature R (first 32 bytes): %s", hex_r);
324   log_debug("Signature S (last 32 bytes): %s", hex_s);
325 
326   return 0;
327 }




References gpg_sign_with_key(), and
  safe_snprintf().


Referenced by ed25519_sign_message().






int
  gpg_sign_with_key (const char * key_id, const uint8_t * message, size_t
  message_len, uint8_t * signature_out, size_t * signature_len_out)


Sign a message using GPG key (via gpg --detach-sign). This
    function uses gpg --detach-sign which internally uses gpg-agent, so no
    passphrase prompt if the key is cached in the agent.




Parameters


key_id GPG key ID (e.g.,
  "7FE90A79F2E80ED3")


message Message to sign


message_len Message length


signature_out Output buffer for signature (caller must provide at least
  512 bytes)


signature_len_out Actual signature length written


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 41 of file signing.c.


42                                                  {
43   if (!key_id || !message || message_len == 0 || !signature_out || !signature_len_out) {
44     log_error("Invalid parameters to gpg_sign_with_key");
45     return -1;
46   }
47 
48   char msg_path[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
49   char sig_path[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
50   int msg_fd = -1;
51   int sig_fd = -1;
52   int result = -1;
53 
54   // Create temp files using platform abstraction
55   if (platform_create_temp_file(msg_path, sizeof(msg_path), "asciichat_msg", &msg_fd) != 0) {
56     log_error("Failed to create temp message file");
57     return -1;
58   }
59 
60   if (platform_create_temp_file(sig_path, sizeof(sig_path), "asciichat_sig", &sig_fd) != 0) {
61     log_error("Failed to create temp signature file");
62     platform_delete_temp_file(msg_path);
63     return -1;
64   }
65 
66   // Close signature file descriptor (will be created by gpg)
67   if (sig_fd >= 0) {
68     close(sig_fd);
69   }
70   platform_delete_temp_file(sig_path); // Remove it so gpg can create it fresh
71 
72   // Write message to temp file
73   ssize_t written = write(msg_fd, message, message_len);
74   close(msg_fd);
75   msg_fd = -1;
76 
77   if (written != (ssize_t)message_len) {
78     log_error("Failed to write message to temp file");
79     goto cleanup;
80   }
81 
82   // Escape key ID for shell command (prevent injection)
83   char escaped_key_id[64];
84   if (!escape_path_for_shell(key_id, escaped_key_id, sizeof(escaped_key_id))) {
85     log_error("Failed to escape GPG key ID for shell command");
86     goto cleanup;
87   }
88 
89   // Call gpg --detach-sign
90   char cmd[BUFFER_SIZE_LARGE];
91   safe_snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd),
92                 "gpg --local-user 0x%s --detach-sign --output \"%s\" \"%s\" " PLATFORM_SHELL_NULL_REDIRECT,
93                 escaped_key_id, sig_path, msg_path);
94 
95   // Note: Don't log the full command as it may contain sensitive key material
96   log_debug("Signing with GPG key 0x%s", escaped_key_id);
97   int status = 0;
98   LOG_IO("gpg", {
99     status = system(cmd);
100   });
101   if (status != 0) {
102     log_error("GPG signing failed (exit code %d)", status);
103     goto cleanup;
104   }
105 
106   // Read signature file
107   FILE *sig_fp = platform_fopen("file_stream", sig_path, "rb");
108   if (!sig_fp) {
109     log_error("Failed to open signature file: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
110     goto cleanup;
111   }
112 
113   fseek(sig_fp, 0, SEEK_END);
114   long sig_size = ftell(sig_fp);
115   fseek(sig_fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
116 
117   if (sig_size <= 0 || sig_size > 512) {
118     log_error("Invalid signature size: %ld bytes", sig_size);
119     fclose(sig_fp);
120     goto cleanup;
121   }
122 
123   size_t bytes_read = fread(signature_out, 1, sig_size, sig_fp);
124   fclose(sig_fp);
125 
126   if (bytes_read != (size_t)sig_size) {
127     log_error("Failed to read signature file");
128     goto cleanup;
129   }
130 
131   *signature_len_out = sig_size;
132   log_debug("GPG signature created successfully (%zu bytes)", *signature_len_out);
133   result = 0;
134 
135 cleanup:
136   if (msg_fd >= 0) {
137     close(msg_fd);
138   }
139   platform_delete_temp_file(msg_path);
140   platform_delete_temp_file(sig_path);
141   return result;
142 }

    platform_fopen(), PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH, and
    safe_snprintf().

    gpg_verify_detached_ed25519().
